items.briefing:
AI is making phishing scams increasingly convincing and harder to spot, with criminals now running highly organised, large-scale phishing operations. At the same time, a China-linked threat group known as TA4922 is expanding its cybercrime activity globally, which may affect Australian organisations. Google is rolling out a new feature on Android phones that detects suspicious calls in real time, which can help protect staff from phone-based scams.

Australia is also a world leader in self-hosted AI use, meaning local organisations may carry greater responsibility for securing their own AI systems and the data within them.

**Practical tip:** If you receive an unexpected call or email asking for login details or payment, verify the request through a separate, known contact method before acting.

featured.title: Australia leads in self-hosted AI use, JFrog finds
featured.quote: Australia's AI adoption outpaces security practices, creating significant vulnerabilities in local organizations.
